answer_explanation: **a.** Fraction of radio (in figure) = 920 Total number of students = 200 ∴ Number of students influenced by radio the most 920 of 200 = 920×200 = 90 **b.** We have to find, (Students influenced by radio) – (Students influenced by music video channel) = (Fraction of radio × Total number of students) – (Fraction of music video channel × Total number of students) = 920 of 200−225 of 200 = 920×200−225×200 = 90 – 16 = 74 Hence, 74 more students were influenced by radio than by a music video channel. **c.** We have to find, (Students who were influenced by a friend or relative) + (Students who were influenced by hearing songs in the shop) = 320 of 200+110 of 200 = 320×200+110×200 = 30 + 20 = 50 shaalaa.com Chapter 2: Fractions and Decimals - Exercise Chapter 2 Fractions and Decimals Exercise | Q 96. | Page 49 #### Video TutorialsVIEW ALL ####

answer_explanation: Length of family photograph = 1425 cm = (14×5)+25 = 725 cm Breadth of family photograph = 1025 cm = (10×5)+25 = 525 cm New length including border (from both sides) = 725+(235×2) = 725+(135×2) = 725+265 = 72+265 = 985 cm New width including border (from both sides) = 525+(235×2) = 525+265 = 52+265 = 785 cm ∴ Area of framed photograph = Length × Breadth = 985×785 = 764425 = 3051925 cm2 Hence, the area of framed photograph is 3051925 cm2. shaalaa.com Problems Based on Decimal Numbers Chapter 2: Fractions and Decimals - Exercise Chapter 2 Fractions and Decimals Exercise | Q 113. | Page 52 ####

question_text: While dividing a fraction by another fraction, we _________ the first fraction by the _______ of the other fraction.
answer_explanation: Cost of 1 burger = ₹ 2034 = ₹ (20×4)+34 = ₹ 834 ∴ Cost of 4 burgers = ₹ 4×834 = ₹ 83 Cost of 1 macpuffs = ₹ 1512 = ₹ 312 Cost of 14 macpuffs = ₹ 14×312 = ₹ 217 ∴ Total cost of 4 burgers and 14 macpuffs = ₹ (83 + 217) = ₹ 300 shaalaa.com Chapter 2: Fractions and Decimals - Exercise Chapter 2 Fractions and Decimals Exercise | Q 114. | Page 52 #### Video TutorialsVIEW ALL ####

subject: Mathematics
answer_explanation: Given, height of the hill = 10113 m = (101×3)+13 = 303+13 = 3043 m ∵ Height of the hill under water = 14 of the height of the hill = 14×3043 = 763 m ∴ Height of the hill above the water = Height of the hill – Height of the hill under water = 3043−763 = 2283 = 76 cm Hence, height of the hill above the water is 76 cm. shaalaa.com #### Solution 2Show Solution Fraction of height of the hill above water = 1−14 = 4−14 = 34 So, 34 of the height of the hill is visible. ∴ Height of the hill above the water = 34 × Height of the hill = 34×10113 = 34×(101×3)+13 = 34×3043 = 76 m shaalaa.com Chapter 2: Fractions and Decimals - Exercise Chapter 2 Fractions and Decimals Exercise | Q 115. | Page 52 Question Bank with Solutions * ") * ") * ") * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * Question Paper Solutions * * * * * * * * * * * * Other Resources * * * * * ") * * * * * * © 2026 Shaalaa.com | | Share 0 0 0 0 0 Notifications #### Select a course CANCEL Login email: password: Log in Course * * * * * * * * * * ×
